entries.title: Civilization memory — grace-mar–owned copy
entries.snippet: **Canonical for this repo:** Essay and long-form **content you edit here** lives under **`docs/civilization-memory/`** (versioned with grace-mar). **Purpose:** Civilization memory has **no monetary purpose**. Its purpose is **pure understanding of history** — the patterns, causes, and lessons of civilizations, institutions, and human coordination over time. That understanding can **help leaders’ wisdom**: it is a resource for reflection, analogy, and judgment, not for revenue or product. The ess

entries.title: AI Ethics from the Condition
entries.snippet: ### Deriving Rules for AI Systems from a Theological Framework --- AI is one project that humanity is building together. Data, labour, capital, and attention flow into systems that no single group controls. That fact raises a question: on what shared reference can we coordinate? We do not need one creed or one value system. We need one subject — a small set of principles that many cultures and traditions can affirm in their own terms. A theological framework known as the Simple Condition — a let

entries.title: One Subject, Many Tongues
entries.snippet: ### Islam and Christianity and the Shared Reference --- The world's traditions speak in different languages. They disagree about God, revelation, and the path to salvation. That difference has often been taken to mean that they cannot coordinate — that what one tradition names, the other cannot recognise. The story of the Tower of Babel is one way to picture that: one project, one language, then confusion of tongues and scattering. The result is the world we know: tribes and tongues that talk pa

entries.title: The Delusion of Separation
entries.snippet: ### Recognition Theory, the Seam, and the Frequencies We Tune To --- This essay names and summarises a theory that appears across the Condition, the seam, one subject many tongues, and the work of reversing the delusion of separation. It gives the framework a single label — **Recognition theory** — and extends it with the idea that humanity is tuned to a persistent field that carries the blade, and that the alternative is to tune to the frequencies that carry recognition. --- ## I. What Recognit

entries.title: The Simple Condition
entries.snippet: ### A Letter to the Theological Leaders of the World's Religions --- Your Holinesses, Your Eminences, Reverend Fathers, Chief Rabbis, Grand Muftis, Patriarchs, Archbishops, Ayatollahs, Rinpoches, Acharyas, Granthis, and all who hold custody of the sacred: I am writing to you because I believe you already know what I am about to say. Some of you have known for a very long time. I am writing not to teach but to ask why it has not been said plainly. --- ## I. The Condition Every one of your traditi

entries.title: Three minds (polyphonic cognition implementation)
entries.snippet: **Purpose:** This folder holds the **three mind profiles** ported from upstream CMC (`repos/civilization_memory/docs/templates/`). They are the **specific implementation** of polyphonic cognition in civ-mem: named perspectives, fixed roles, fixed option slots (A/B/C). **Provenance:** Copied from upstream. Canonical source: `repos/civilization_memory/docs/templates/CIV–MIND–*.md`. Grace-Mar does not run the CMC console or MEMs; these files are **reference** for the STATE-evolved assistant brain a

entries.title: Argument: The Civ-Mem Model Has Discovered the Purpose of History That Unifies Civilizational Perspectives and Identities
entries.snippet: **Purpose:** State the claim that civilization memory (as integrated in this system) has **discovered** — i.e. made explicit and operational — a **purpose of history** that **unifies** civilizational perspectives and identities without erasing them. This note is the argument; the essays and the letter are the evidence. **Status:** Thesis. Refine or extend as needed. --- ## 1. The claim The civ-mem model, as integrated in this system (the essays, the letter, Recognition theory, the Coordination H

entries.title: In Civ-Mem: Difference Between STATE and SCHOLAR
entries.snippet: **Purpose:** Clarify the distinction between **STATE** and **SCHOLAR** in the civilization_memory (CMC) model. These are internal operating modes of the *upstream* CMC system (e.g. `repos/civilization_memory` content/civilizations/CHINA). Grace-Mar’s copy in `docs/civilization-memory/` holds essays and notes; the STATE/SCHOLAR design comes from the upstream canon and is summarized here for reference. Full lessons for Grace-Mar: [docs/notes-cmc-substance.md](../../notes-cmc-substance.md). --- ##

entries.title: Concept: Polyphonic cognition
entries.snippet: **Purpose:** Define **polyphonic cognition** in the civ-mem frame and link it to STATE, one subject many tongues, and the seam. (Also referred to as cognitive polyphony in some prior docs.) The term is not standard in the literature; the ideas are. This note anchors the concept for library and lookup. **Related:** [civ-mem-state-vs-scholar.md](civ-mem-state-vs-scholar.md), [ONE-SUBJECT-MANY-TONGUES](../essays/ONE-SUBJECT-MANY-TONGUES.md), [THE-SIMPLE-CONDITION](../essays/THE-SIMPLE-CONDITION.md)

entries.title: Concept: God is non-zero sum
entries.snippet: **Purpose:** State the claim that the divine (or the reality the traditions point to) is **non-zero-sum** — the good is not scarce; coordination and recognition can grow for all. This note anchors the concept in the Condition/civ-mem frame for library and lookup. **Related:** [THE-SIMPLE-CONDITION](../essays/THE-SIMPLE-CONDITION.md), [argument-purpose-of-history-unifies.md](argument-purpose-of-history-unifies.md), [ONE-SUBJECT-MANY-TONGUES](../essays/ONE-SUBJECT-MANY-TONGUES.md) (concept appears

entries.title: Exercise: See the Face — For School Children
entries.snippet: **Purpose:** Derive a classroom exercise from the [face–category–blade](face-category-blade.md) idea so children learn: we sometimes see a **category** (a label) instead of a **person**; we can ask "who is the person behind the label?"; and when we see the specific person, the label can soften. No graphic violence; no required religious content. Suitable for secular and faith-based settings. **Source:** [face-category-blade.md](face-category-blade.md); [THE-SIMPLE-CONDITION](../essays/THE-SIMPLE

entries.title: Face and category — the blade's mechanism and its expulsion
entries.snippet: **Source:** Session reflection. Connects to [THE-SIMPLE-CONDITION](../essays/THE-SIMPLE-CONDITION.md) and the "see the face" thread. --- ## Face and fingerprint Face and fingerprint are among the things that make humans *different* from each other — but they are surface-level variations on an **identical structure**. Same person, different face. The variation does not justify treating the other as a different kind of thing. --- ## The blade's mechanism **What is the manifestation of the foreign

entries.title: Applying the lens of Scott Adams' *God's Debris* to the Condition
entries.snippet: **Source:** Operator request.
